title: celtis laevigata var. texana
intro: Celtis laevigata var. texana, also known as the Texas hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Celtis laevigata var. texana include its ability to support digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often used in conjunction with other herbs to create a range of medicinal products.
